text:
- Update to PR, add check for staticlib atlas lib if atlas is used
  otherwise the port will not link correctly if atlas is used.
  This duplicates the same test found in py-scipy and py-scipy03
- port needs PORTEPOCH because of earlier committed 1.0b3, portlint complains:
  FATAL: Makefile: py24-numpy-1.0 < py24-numpy-1.0b3.

echo c - py-numpy
mkdir -p py-numpy > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - py-numpy/files
mkdir -p py-numpy/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - py-numpy/files/site.cfg
sed 's/^X//' >py-numpy/files/site.cfg << 'END-of-py-numpy/files/site.cfg'
X[atlas]
Xlibrary_dirs = /usr/local/lib
Xatlas_libs = alapack_r, f77blas_r, cblas_r, atlas_r
END-of-py-numpy/files/site.cfg

X+++ numpy/core/include/numpy/ufuncobject.h	Sat Dec  9 22:51:47 2006

X /* Solaris --------------------------------------------------------*/
X /* --------ignoring SunOS ieee_flags approach, someone else can
X **         deal with that! */
X-#elif defined(sun) || defined(__BSD__) || defined(__OpenBSD__) || defined(__FreeBSD__) || defined(__NetBSD__)

X+#include <sys/param.h>
X+#endif
X+#elif defined(sun) || defined(__BSD__) || defined(__OpenBSD__) || (defined(__FreeBSD__) && (__FreeBSD_version < 502114)) || defined(__NetBSD__)
X #include <ieeefp.h>
X 
X #define UFUNC_CHECK_STATUS(ret) {				\
X@@ -276,9 +279,9 @@
X 	(void) fpsetsticky(0);						\
X 	}
X 
X-#elif defined(linux) || defined(__APPLE__) || defined(__CYGWIN__) || defined(__MINGW32__)

X 
X-#if defined(__GLIBC__) || defined(__APPLE__) || defined(__MINGW32__)

X--- numpy/distutils/fcompiler/__init__.py.orig	Wed Oct 11 22:28:42 2006
X+++ numpy/distutils/fcompiler/__init__.py	Sat Dec  9 22:51:47 2006
X@@ -273,11 +273,7 @@
X         f90 = self.__get_cmd('compiler_f90','F90',(conf,'f90exec'))
X         # Temporarily setting f77,f90 compilers so that
X         # version_cmd can use their executables.
X-        if f77:
X-            self.set_executables(compiler_f77=[f77])
X-        if f90:
X-            self.set_executables(compiler_f90=[f90])
X-
X+	# If you do that, --f77exec and --f90exec break -db
X         # Must set version_cmd before others as self.get_flags*
X         # methods may call self.get_version.
X         vers_cmd = self.__get_cmd(self.get_version_cmd)
END-of-py-numpy/files/patch-numpy-distutils-fcompiler-__init__.py

X+++ numpy/distutils/system_info.py	Sat Dec  9 22:51:47 2006
X@@ -860,6 +860,8 @@
X         atlas_1 = None
X         for d in lib_dirs:
X             atlas = self.check_libs2(d,atlas_libs,[])
X+            if atlas is not None:
X+                atlas['libraries'].extend(['g2c', 'm', 'pthread'])
X             lapack_atlas = self.check_libs2(d,['lapack_atlas'],[])
X             if atlas is not None:
X                 lib_dirs2 = [d] + self.combine_paths(d,['atlas*','ATLAS*'])

X# New ports collection makefile for:	numpy
X# Date created:				24 July 2006
X# Whom:					Tony Maher
X#
X# $FreeBSD: ports/math/py-numpy/Makefile,v 1.3 2006/11/15 05:50:39 rafan Exp $
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	numpy
XPORTVERSION=	1.0
XPORTEPOCH=	1
XCATEGORIES=	math python
XMASTER_SITES=	http://numpy.scipy.org/:doc \
X		${MASTER_SITE_SOURCEFORGE:S/$/:source/}
XMASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=	numpy/:source
XPKGNAMEPREFIX=	${PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX}
XDISTFILES=	numpy-${PORTVERSION:S/r/rc/}${EXTRACT_SUFX}:source \
X		numpy.pdf:doc
XEXTRACT_ONLY=	numpy-${PORTVERSION:S/r/rc/}${EXTRACT_SUFX}
X
XMAINTAINER=	db@db.net
XCOMMENT=	The New Numeric Extension to Python
X
XCONFLICTS=	f2py-*
X
XOPTIONS=	ATLAS "Use optimized blas library" Off
X
XUSE_PYTHON=	yes
XUSE_PYDISTUTILS=yes
XMAN1=		f2py.1
X
XLATEST_LINK=	py-${PORTNAME}
XWRKSRC=	${WRKDIR}/${DISTNAME:S/r/rc/}
X
X.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
X
X.if defined(WITH_ATLAS)
XLIB_DEPENDS+=	atlas.1:${PORTSDIR}/math/atlas
X.if !exists(${LOCALBASE}/lib/libalapack.a)
XIGNORE=	atlas needs to be built with WITH_STATICLIB for numpy to function properly
X.endif
X.else
XLIB_DEPENDS+=	lapack.3:${PORTSDIR}/math/lapack \
X		blas.1:${PORTSDIR}/math/blas
X.endif
X
X.if ${OSVERSION} < 500000
XBROKEN=		does not compile
X.endif
X
Xpre-fetch:
X.if ${PYTHON_REL} < 200
X	@${ECHO} "Py-numpy doesn't work with Python versions < 2.0."
X	@${ECHO} "Please use the py-numeric17 port for these."
X	@${FALSE}
X.endif
X
Xpost-extract:
X	@${CP} ${DISTDIR}/numpy.pdf ${WRKDIR}
X
Xpost-patch:
X.if defined(WITH_ATLAS)
X	@${CP} ${FILESDIR}/site.cfg ${WRKSRC}
X.endif
X
Xpost-install:
X	${INSTALL_MAN} ${WRKSRC}/numpy/f2py/f2py.1 ${MAN1PREFIX}/man/man1
X.if !defined(NOPORTDOCS)
X	@${MKDIR} ${DOCSDIR}
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKDIR}/numpy.pdf ${DOCSDIR}
X.endif
X
X.include <bsd.port.post.mk>
END-of-py-numpy/Makefile

XThe fundamental package needed for scientific computing with Python is
Xcalled NumPy.  This package contains:
X
X    * a powerful N-dimensional array object
X    * sophisticated (broadcasting) functions
X    * basic linear algebra functions
X    * basic Fourier transforms
X    * sophisticated random number capabilities
X    * tools for integrating Fortran code.
X
XNumPy derives from the old Numeric code base and can be used as a
Xreplacement for Numeric.   It also adds the features introduced by numarray
Xand can also be used to replace numarray.
X
XNote:	Development for Numeric has ceased, and users should transisition to
X	NumPy as quickly as possible.
X
XWWW: http://numpy.scipy.org/
END-of-py-numpy/pkg-descr
